visual: In a horse stance, one hand traces a continuous spiraling circle in front of the body driven by the waist, the palm rotating smoothly throughout.
motion_description: From a wide stance, the right arm traces a slow, continuous vertical circle in front of the body while the left hand rests near the left hip. Beginning at face height on the left side with the palm facing inward, the right hand sweeps downward to waist level as the weight shifts to the right leg and the torso turns right, with the palm rotating to face upward. The right arm then arcs up to shoulder height on the right side and sweeps across to the starting position as the weight shifts back to the left leg and the torso turns left, the palm rotating to face left.
